# 🔒 Word Devs — Security Policy

<div align="center">

<img src="../Profile/Assets/Images/Banners/security-banner.png" alt="Word Devs Security Policy" width="100%">

    ( •_•)>⌐■-■

**Let's keep Word Devs safe and sound!**
_We take security seriously and appreciate your help in keeping our community secure_ 💜✨

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

</div>

We want to make sure all of our users, developers, and projects are safe. If you discover a security vulnerability in any of our repositories, bots, or packages, please let us know as soon as possible through a private report. 🌸

Please do **NOT** open a public issue for security vulnerabilities. Let's work together to fix it privately!

---

## 🛡️ Supported Versions

We actively support and patch security issues in the latest major releases of our core projects. Please make sure you are always using the most up-to-date version!

| 📦 Project                       | 🟢 Supported Versions |
| :------------------------------- | :-------------------- |
| **Kannamy** (Discord Bot)        | Latest release        |
| **cf-keys** (JWT/Crypto Toolkit) | Latest release        |
| **kira-arts** (Card Generator)   | Latest release        |

---

## 📧 Reporting a Vulnerability

If you find a security bug or potential leak, please report it privately:

1.  **Do not** open a public GitHub Issue or public Pull Request about it.
2.  Send a detailed email explaining the issue to:
    👉 **[team@chocofactory.dev](mailto:team@chocofactory.dev)** 💌
3.  Please include:
    - A description of the vulnerability and its potential impact.
    - Detailed steps or a proof-of-concept (PoC) code to reproduce the issue.
    - The affected project name and its version.

We will review your email privately and get back to you as quickly as possible (usually within 24–48 hours) to coordinate a fix.

---

## 🌸 Our Commitment

If you report a vulnerability responsibly, we promise to:

- Acknowledge receipt of your report promptly.
- Work with you to understand and resolve the issue.
- Give you proper credit in our release notes once the patch is published (unless you prefer to remain anonymous!).
